topics: 👂 חרש שוטה וקטן: 'פגיעתן רעה' — bad to encounter
👋 The fixed price list: סלע / מנה / מאתיים / ארבע מאות זוז
👑 R' Akiva: even the poorest are בני אברהם יצחק ויעקב
💄 The flask trick — חובל בעצמו פטור, אחרים חייבים
🙏 Forgiveness: אינו נמחל לו עד שיבקש ממנו

overview.title: 📖 Overview — Honor, Insult, and Forgiveness
overview.label: The sugya:
overview.text: Daf 36 spans three Mishnayot. First: vulnerable parties (deaf-mute, minor, woman, slave) — פגיעתן רעה: dangerous to meet. Second: the famous fixed-price list for slaps and indignities, R' Akiva's ruling that all Jews are aristocrats, and the trickster who broke an oil flask to expose a woman's hair. Third: even after paying, the offender is not forgiven until he asks — and the wronged party may not be cruel.
overview.hebrewSource: אַף עַל פִּי שֶׁהוּא נוֹתֵן לוֹ אֵינוֹ נִמְחָל לוֹ עַד שֶׁיְּבַקֵּשׁ מִמֶּנּוּ שֶׁנֶּאֱמַר וְעַתָּה הָשֵׁב אֵשֶׁת הָאִישׁ כִּי נָבִיא הוּא

slides.title: 👋 The Fixed Price List for Indignities
slides.subtitle: The Mishnah quantifies each kind of public humiliation.
rows: סֶלַע (R' Yehuda b'shem R' Yose haGalili: מָנֶה)
Earlobe / hair / spit / removed garment / uncovered woman's hair

callout.label: Key principle:
callout.text: הַכֹּל לְפִי כְבוֹדוֹ — but R' Akiva pushes back: every Jew is an aristocrat by birth.

slides.title: 💄 The Flask of Oil — A Famous Trick
slides.subtitle: How a defendant tried to prove a woman's 'honor' was already cheap.

steps.content: 1️⃣ A man uncovered a woman's hair; R' Akiva ruled אַרְבַּע מֵאוֹת זוּז.
2️⃣ The defendant asks for time to pay — and gets it.
3️⃣ He waits at her courtyard and breaks a flask of oil (worth an אִיסָּר) before her.
4️⃣ She uncovers her own hair to gather the oil — he plants witnesses.
5️⃣ He returns to R' Akiva: 'Should I really pay 400 זוז to this woman?'
6️⃣ R' Akiva: הַחוֹבֵל בְּעַצְמוֹ אַף עַל פִּי שֶׁאֵינוֹ רַשַּׁאי פָּטוּר וַאֲחֵרִים שֶׁחָבְלוּ בוֹ חַיָיבִים — self-injury exempt, others liable.

callout.label: Bottom line:
callout.text: Compare to הַקּוֹצֵץ אֶת נְטִיעוֹתָיו — cutting your own trees: not allowed, but no liability. Others who cut them are liable. Honor works the same way.

slides.title: 🤐 בושת and Verbal Insult
slides.subtitle: Three sub-rulings the Yerushalmi attaches to the price list.

boxes.title: 💧 Spit That Misses
items: Question: how far must it reach? body? clothes?

boxes.title: 👴 Insulting a זקן
items: Reish Lakish: הַמְבַיֵישׁ אֶת הַזָּקֵן נוֹתֵן לוֹ דְמֵי בוֹשְׁתּוֹ מֻשְׁלַם
Story: someone insulted R' Yehuda b. Chanina
Reish Lakish fined him a לִיטְרָא דִדְהַב — pound of gold

boxes.title: ❓ 'I Don't Know' Defense
items: R' Yochanan: still חייב — when witnesses testify

callout.label: Practical reading:
callout.text: The Yerushalmi inserts these halakhot inside the bigger Mishnah on indignities — verbal, gestural, and accusatorial all get separate treatment.

slides.title: 👑 R' Akiva on the Dignity of Every Jew
slides.subtitle: The Mishnah's most quoted line — and its theological core.

lines.speaker: M
lines.text: Anonymous Mishnah: הַכֹּל לְפִי כְבוֹדוֹ — payment scales with the victim's status.

lines.speaker: RA
lines.text: R' Akiva: אֲפִילוּ עָנִי שֶׁבְּיִשְׂרָאֵל רוֹאִין אוֹתָן כְּאִילּוּ הֵן בְּנֵי חוֹרִין שֶׁיָּרְדוּ מִנִּכְסֵיהֶן — even the poorest is treated as a fallen aristocrat.

lines.speaker: RA
lines.text: Reason: שֶׁהֵן בְּנֵי אַבְרָהָם יִצְחָק וְיַעֲקֹב.

lines.speaker: Y
lines.text: Application: in the woman's case, R' Akiva imposed the full 400 זוז regardless of her socioeconomic standing.

callout.label: Why it matters:
callout.text: R' Akiva democratizes the price list. Honor is not aristocratic privilege but Israelite birthright.

slides.title: 🙏 The Mishnah on Forgiveness
slides.subtitle: Even after paying, the offender is not yet forgiven.

nodes.content: Mishnah: אַף עַל פִּי שֶׁהוּא נוֹתֵן לוֹ אֵינוֹ נִמְחָל לוֹ
Source 1: וְעַתָּה הָשֵׁב אֵשֶׁת הָאִישׁ כִּי נָבִיא הוּא — Avimelech must ask Avraham

columns.label: If refused
columns.content: Rav: יַעֲשֶׂה שׁוּרַת בְּנֵי אָדָם וִיפַיְיסֶנּוּ — assemble a row to ask again

columns.label: Wronged party
columns.content: שֶׁלֹּא יְהֵא הַמּוֹחֵל אַכְזָרִי — must not be cruel

nodes.content: R' Yose: הוֹצִיא לוֹ שֵׁם רַע אֵין לוֹ מְחִילָה עוֹלָמִית — defamation has no eternal pardon

callout.label: Bottom line:
callout.text: Job's friends model the cycle: prayer for the offender brings the prayer's own restoration — וַיי שָׁב אֶת שְׁבוּת אִיּוֹב בְּהִתְפַּלְּלוֹ בְּעַד רֵעֵהוּ.

summary.principle: Bodily insult is governed by a fixed price list — but R' Akiva universalizes the dignity, the flask story limits it, and the chapter closes with the moral architecture of forgiveness.

flowSteps.content:
Vulnerable parties
חרש, שוטה, קטן, אשה, עבד — פגיעתן רעה
Then: the fixed price list for indignities

🎓 Key Principle:
Money closes the legal claim; only words close the moral one. וְעַתָּה הָשֵׁב אֵשֶׁת הָאִישׁ.

quiz.q: What does the Mishnah mean by פְּגִיעָתָן רָעָה?
o: Their crops are bad
It is bad to encounter them — they can be injured but cannot pay if they injure
They are deemed evil people
They are exempt from the Torah
quiz.e: הַחוֹבֵל בָּהֶן חַיָיב וְהֵם שֶׁחָבְלוּ בַּאֲחֵרִים פְּטוּרִין — anyone injuring them pays, but they themselves do not.

quiz.q: What does the Mishnah say is the payment for a backhand slap (לְאַחַר יָדוֹ)?
o: סֶלַע
200 זוּז
400 זוּז
לִיטְרָא דִדְהַב
quiz.e: Mishnah: סְתָרוֹ נוֹתֵן לוֹ מָאתַיִם זוּז לְאַחַר יָדוֹ נוֹתֵן לוֹ אַרְבַּע מֵאוֹת זוּז. Backhand is the most insulting.

quiz.q: What is R' Akiva's foundational principle for indignity payments?
o: Only the rich receive the full 400 זוז
Even the poorest in Israel are treated as fallen aristocrats — בְּנֵי אַבְרָהָם יִצְחָק וְיַעֲקֹב
All payments must be split with the court
Embarrassment is paid only when there is also physical injury
quiz.e: אֲפִילוּ עָנִי שֶׁבְּיִשְׂרָאֵל רוֹאִין אוֹתָן כְּאִילּוּ הֵן בְּנֵי חוֹרִין שֶׁיָּרְדוּ מִנִּכְסֵיהֶן.

quiz.q: Why did R' Akiva reject the defendant's flask-of-oil ruse?
o: Because the witnesses were not valid
Because הַחוֹבֵל בְּעַצְמוֹ פָּטוּר וַאֲחֵרִים שֶׁחָבְלוּ בוֹ חַיָיבִים — self-injury exempts the actor, not others
Because he had already paid
Because R' Akiva had changed his ruling
quiz.e: Just like cutting your own fruit trees: not permitted but not actionable; others who do the same are liable.

quiz.q: What does the closing Mishnah teach about forgiveness?
o: Once you pay, you are automatically forgiven
אֵינוֹ נִמְחָל לוֹ עַד שֶׁיְּבַקֵּשׁ מִמֶּנּוּ — you must also ask, and the wronged party must not be cruel
Forgiveness is impossible for any injury
Only Yom Kippur grants forgiveness
quiz.e: Source: וְעַתָּה הָשֵׁב אֵשֶׁת הָאִישׁ כִּי נָבִיא הוּא. R' Yose adds that defamation has no eternal pardon.
